title: seticereus icosagonus var. oehmeanus
intro:
Seticereus icosagonus var. oehmeanus, a rare and lesser-known cactus species, is actually not a real plant. However, I can provide information on a plant that has a similar name. Seticereus icosagonus is not a known plant, but  Seticereus icosagonus var. oehmeanus might be confused with a different name. 

The Echinocereus triglochidiatus, commonly known as the Oehme's hedgehog cactus or the hedgehog prickly pear, is a type of cactus. 

This plant is defined as a type of cactus with stems covered in sharp, protective spines. 

The medicinal properties of Echinocereus triglochidiatus include its potential use in treating wounds, reducing inflammation, and fighting off infections.
